articles.title: One Billion Reasons to Fill Out a Bracket
articles.author: Ryan Lu
articles.dek: Prediction market Kalshi is offering a billion-dollar prize for a perfect NCAA bracket. Lincoln students are skeptical — but optimistic.
articles.body:
March Madness 2026 has officially begun, bringing the annual excitement of college basketball's biggest tournament along with a new level of attention on brackets. The 68-team NCAA tournament started after Selection Sunday on March 15 and the main games tipped off March 19, beginning three weeks of single elimination matchups that will end with the national championship in early April.

At the center of the tournament is the bracket, where fans predict the outcome of every game from the first round all the way to the championship. Filling out a bracket has become one of the most popular traditions in sports, even though predicting every game correctly is nearly impossible. A perfect bracket requires picking all 63 games correctly, something that has never been done, and the odds are often compared to winning the lottery or being struck by lightning.

\"Expect the unexpected.\" The odds of a perfect bracket are 1 in 9.2 quintillion — yet nearly 30% of Lincoln students still believe it could happen.

This year, brackets are getting more attention than ever because of a massive prize. Prediction market platform Kalshi is offering one billion dollars to anyone who can create a perfect bracket for the 2026 tournament. The contest is free to enter, limited to one bracket per person, and requires correctly predicting all 63 games. If no one achieves a perfect bracket, the best overall entry will still win one million dollars.

What Lincoln Students Think

A survey conducted by the Railsplitter highlights just how unlikely most people believe a perfect bracket really is. Out of 73 responses, 52 Lincoln students (71.2%) said they do not believe a perfect bracket will be achieved in their lifetime, while only 21 respondents (28.8%) said they think it is possible.

Why March Madness Endures

Even so, the fact that nearly 30% of respondents still believe it could happen shows the optimism and excitement that define March Madness. With millions of brackets filled out each year and growing incentives like the billion-dollar prize, fans continue to hope that someday, someone might finally beat the odds.

As the tournament's saying goes: expect the unexpected.

articles.title: Untitled Test Article
articles.author: RYAN Lu
articles.dek: Test123
articles.body:
Kanye West has officially released BULLY, a long-delayed and heavily scrutinized album that arrives during one of the most turbulent periods of his career. The project, his twelfth studio album, finally reached streaming services on March 28th, after months of missed release dates, leaks, and public revisions. The album was delayed for approximately 309 days.

BULLY features a wide range of collaborators, including Travis Scott and CeeLo Green, and blends alternative hip-hop, industrial rap, and neo-soul influences. The album also reflects West’s recent experimentation with Retrieval-based Voice Conversion (RVC) synthesis, with earlier, leaked versions of the album reportedly featuring AI-generated vocals that were later reworked, adding another layer of intrigue and skepticism to its rollout.

Early reactions have been divided. Critics describe BULLY as technically ambitious but uneven; an album shaped as much by controversy as creativity. Some praise its production and sonic range, while others view it as fragmented and lacking the cohesion of his earlier work. However, the album’s arrival is inseparable from the headlines surrounding West himself. In recent months, he has faced significant backlash across Europe, where concerts tied to the album rollout have been canceled or postponed. Officials and promoters have pointed to his past statements and behavior as justification, complicating what would otherwise be a major global return tied to BULLY.

articles.title: The $400,000 Admission Letter
articles.author: Samantha Ortiz
articles.dek: Lincoln NAI students are accepted to USC. By Ryan Lu
articles.body:
The AP and Magnet awards are presented to those schools that have demonstrated excellent performance in test scores and education. Information from the LAUSD website states that the MSA’s National Merit awards program honors the schools that demonstrated their ability to improve student academic achievement, “promote racial and socioeconomic diversity, provide integrated curricula and instruction, and create strong family and community partnerships that enhance the school’s magnet theme.” Abraham Lincoln High School received the Magnet School of Excellence award for exhibiting the utmost excellence in all aspects of the merit award application. The AP School Honor Roll recognizes the outstanding results delivered by schools’ AP programs. To reach this recognition, schools must show commitment to college going-culture, supply students with the possibility to earn college credit and college readiness. Gold, silver and bronze are the four different levels the Honor Roll presents. Lincoln High school earned the Silver AP Honor Roll for the 2025 school year because their AP Programs met all those requirements.

The final week of January brought a wave of cardinal and gold to Lincoln High School as the University of Southern California (USC) released its Early Action decisions for the Class of 2030. For a dedicated group of seniors, the "Congratulations" notification on the applicant portal represented the culmination of years of academic rigor. The success of these students is closely tied to the Neighborhood Academic Initiative (NAD, a partnership between USC and local schools like Lincoln. The program requires a seven-year commitment, involving daily morning classes at the USC Health Sciences Campus and mandatory Saturday Academy sessions. This year, the results of that persistence are visible in the high number of Lincoln students securing spots at one of the most competitive private universities in the country. While USC’s general acceptance rate has remained under 10% for the 2026 cycle, the NAI program significantly shifts the odds for Lincoln students. The program provides a structured pathway that emphasizes college readiness, specialized tutoring, and family workshops. Students who successfully complete the NAI requirements and meet USC’s admission standards are not only accepted at higher rates but also receive a financial package that changes the trajectory of their future. The financial incentive remains the cornerstone of the NAI program. For the 2026-2027 academic year, the total cost of attendance at USC has reached record highs, making private education nearly impossible for many families without significant aid. However, NAI scholars who gain admission to USC receive a full-tuition scholarship. When combined with federal and state grants, this often covers the entire cost of housing and books, allowing Lincoln students to graduate from a top-tier university debt-free. With the Early Action cycle now concluded, the focus at Lincoln shifts toward the remaining months of senior year and the upcoming transition to university life. The success of this year’s NAI class reinforces the long-standing bond between Lincoln and USC, ensuring that the path from Lincoln to a world-class education remains open for the next generation of students.

Abraham Lincoln High School

articles.title: Cooking Club Mixes Learning and Tasty Treats
articles.author: Katherine Jimenez
articles.dek: Multiple Pathways to Success After High School
articles.body:
Andre Chen, Yawen Liang, Jieya Liu The JV team consists of Sandra Portillo, Xinyue Huang, David Lin, Jachym Taussig. The ACADECA competition took place at Garfield High School, where students gathered in a large room filled with competitors from other schools. Each academic test lasted 30 minutes, with approximately 50 questions per subject. Later, students faced one of the most personal parts of the competition: interviews and speeches, where judges evaluated not just knowledge, but confidence, communication, and character. Abby Guerra, current Scholastic member, shared her thoughts after the competition. “I have full confidence in our team securing some medals,” Abby said. “Jiali is the second highest scorer in all of LAUSD from scrimmages alone.” When asked about making it to state, Abby didn’t hesitate. “Of course. The team has prepared rigorously and with all their might.” But for Abby, state isn’t just about medals. “State is going to be really fun. Last year, everyone was friends with everyone. It felt like a family. There was love all around. It brought us closer, and we kept hanging out all year. As the team looks ahead to state competitions later this year, the coaches and students alike are excited not just for the chance to win, but for one more opportunity to learn, grow, and experience something special together.

Need something to do after school? Join The Educare Cooking Club where you can create tasty, easy recipes, hang out with friends, and become a pro chef! No experience needed... just bring your creativity! They will be teaching how to make healthy nutritional food and teach a basic skill to students. Any student can join from 9-12, and it runs every Thursday and Friday after school in B17. Students partake in working together to make a wide range of meals and light snacks, from chocolate covered fruit, burrito bowls, and testing different egg substitutes for baked goods like brownies. In the cooking club sometimes when working in groups each student will have an assigned task to make a meal, but there will be times when you can or will work independently to make a meal on your own. The Railsplitter talked to Christopher Garcia, a junior and vice president who frequents the club. When asked what interests him about the club, he said, “What I like about the Cooking Club is that we learn to make meals we have never made before or tried.” Chipotle inspired burrito bowls were on the menu one day, and they were tasty, healthy and are still his favorite meal they’ve made. The Cooking Club is a great option for students staying after school, and it provides a fun, hands-on environment to develop an essential skill for any upcoming circumstances like being home alone and you can cook up something for yourself on your own. Students will master skills in chopping, baking and measuring while learning to follow a recipe. This skill also can boost your creativity, increase your confidence by preparing something from scratch, and even promote teamwork by improving your communication. It gives students the opportunity to meet new people and explore their interest in cooking, and not only will they learn and gain cooking skills, but they will also bring home and share their delicious homemade creations with their friends and families.

One of the biggest and challenging decisions seniors face is figuring out what to do after high school. Graduation ends one chapter of your life and starts a new one with new opportunities. While some plan to attend a four year university, others may choose to attend a trade school, community college, military or just go straight to work. For a lot of students going to college seems like the next step in life, but roughly 4 out of 10 students are unclear about their career expectations, double the number from about a decade ago according to Educational Week Magazine.. Aaron Perez is currently a senior who plans on attending USC and studying law. “I plan on dorming at USC and I'm also trying to be a lawyer.” Going to a four year university is a great way to set yourself up for future success but it's not the only path to success. Trade schools and community colleges are also great options. Other Seniors like Kevin Arce and Davis Cervantes plan to attend Los Angeles Trade Tech where Kevin is attending for carpentry and Davis is attending for plumbing. By considering interests, financial realities, and resources, students can make a choice that feels right for them. Remember, the next chapter of life after high school is about learning, exploring, and building a future, and whatever path you choose has your own personal choice and value stamped on it. If you believe in yourself, any pathway is a pathway to success.

articles.title: Lincoln News So Where Did Al] Safety Outside of School
articles.author: Qimei Li
articles.body:
a o Some students from Lincoln report that they feel unsafe when walking to school or going home because they encounter aggressive people. Case in point: On the morning of a February 10, a man carrying a long stick was seen wandering around near 7/11. A student, Xiaoqi said, “I was waiting at the traffic light and I heard some arguing and _ ~~ : z fae fighting sounds from that man and another person. I was on my phone, so didn’t pay ‘ much attention. Then I noticed the same person holding a long stick and walking along By Ryan Lu the same path as me.” According to the student, this doesn’t happen every day, but she and her friends have encountered odd behavior on the streets several times this semester. A few weeks ago, there was a person carrying a knife on the street outside the campus. Ms. Gomez, Mr. Sanchez, and the security team kept students away and safe. When asked about how schools deal with this type of situation, the principal, Ms. Gomez said, “The first thing is to report the issue and the administration will investigate to make sure the campus is safe.” The principal and staff made sure students walking home and on the bus were away from the person carrying the knife. A report was made to LA school police and they investigated. Ms. Gomez also said she wrote a document and sent out a parent portal call as well as sent an email to families. The Railsplitter survey on walking to and from school reveals an above average rate of feeling unsafe with a 3.5 on a scale from | to 5 ( five being feeling very unsafe). Lincoln Heights has a dense population. When walking to and from school, it’s best to walk with a friend. Try not to be on the phone and pay attention to surroundings when walking alone.

Parking has become an ongoing concern for students at Lincoln. Without a designated school parking lot, seniors and juniors who are able to drive must depend entirely on street parking in the surrounding neighborhood. Weekly city regulations further limit the already small number of available spaces. On Tuesdays, parking is prohibited on one side of the street due to scheduled street sweeping. On Wednesdays, the restriction shifts to the opposite side. For two consecutive days, students lose access to much of the nearby parking. A violation results in approximately a 70 dollar ticket, which is a significant expense. Despite these strict rules, several students report that the streets often appear unchanged and that they rarely observe active street cleaning during the restricted hours. Parking is also limited on Mondays because trash collection occupies curb space. As a result, Thursday and . . Friday are typically the only days without major restrictions. Even on those days, parking fills quickly, forcing A N Ig ht at th e T h eatre : Watc h ] n g many students to park several blocks away and walk to campus. When interviewed, a senior said, “It gets really hard to find parking on Tuesdays and Wednesdays. Sometimes the closest spot is three blocks away, and it takes about ten minutes to walk to campus.” When they S IX at H ol lywood Pa ntag es were asked to rate the difficulty on a scale from 1 (easy to find parking close to school) to 10 (almost impossible to find any parking), they said it is usually around 6 to 7 on street-sweeping days. The limited availability of parking adds stress to the school day and increases the risk of tardiness. Until a long term solution is considered, students at Lincoln High School will continue to navigate a challenging and competitive parking situation.

The trip gave students a chance to experience live theater and learn more about history in a fun and modern way. SIX is a musical about the six wives of King Henry VIII. articles.body: After the show, the Railsplitter interviewed Mia Olmos, a senior and part of SCM, about her experience. Mia said, “I didn’t know much about it, but I have seen some scenes and songs from my childhood.” After watching the full performance, she was able to understand the story better and see how each queen had her own “unique personality and struggles.” When I asked which queen stood out to her the most, Mia said, Catherine Howard, the Pink Queen. She explained that Catherine Howard was looking for love, but people only wanted her for lust. Mia said that when Catherine thought she finally found love, it wasn’t what she expected it to be. This made her story emotional and powerful. Mia said her favorite moment was when Anne Boleyn, the Green Queen, made a joke about her beheading and turned it into a funny, but shocking joke. That moment stood out because it mixed humor with history in a bold way. Overall, the field trip to see SIX was a fun and meaningful experience for SCM students. The musical shared important history in a modern style that made it easier to understand and enjoy. Many students left the theater entertained, inspired, and thinking differently about the stories of these queens.

Tigers, it’s that season again! The time when our seniors get all dressed up to go out for their one night out when they can have fun and make memories after all of the hard work they’ve done over the course of the past four years! The class of 2026 had their prom reveal on February 27 in the auditorium during advisory, but before the reveal. Seniors Alvin Stricklen said, “I might go to prom but I think the theme is gonna be Miami.” We asked him what he meant by that and he replied with “Nights in Miami.” Edwin Dolores said, “I’m not going to prom because I just don’t feel like it, but I think it’s gonna be Rapunzel.” Seyha Keo and Justin Cain said, “Mascare.” Seyha does plan on attending prom, but Justin does not. When it was finally revealed, The Class of 2026 Prom Theme was ...The Upper East Side, New York! Ladies and Gents, if you aren’t too sure what this theme might mean | advise you to seriously watch Gossip Girls. The theme is based around old money and the very classy Upper East Side of New York!

Do you often get to school wanting to drink or eat something, but don’t have the time to grab a snack before school? Well, worry no more! Say thanks to your new Tiger Den Cafe located in room 111. The Tiger Den Cafe is a spot where students can get their drinks and is run by the AUT.core (Autism Core Program) student group. The group's purpose is to provide an opportunity for students to learn life and social skills, and it also helps raise money for the senior activities this year. They have a variety of drinks including Americano, tea, latte, chocomilk, pink tiger, strawberry acai, and to eat they have bagels, muffins, and coffee cake. Almost all of these options have different choices like the flavor or if you want your coffee hot or cold. There is something about meeting the people behind the cafe that is very special. The teachers, students, and other people that help out are very nice when taking your orders and making the drinks. The profit has been lucrative with Ms. Ramirez saying, “We [were able to] fundraise at the football games and we were able to raise enough funds to be able to purchase our machine and other items that we were missing.” The menu is classic coffee shop style, which adds to the overall experience. The process for deciding the menu items “was a collaborative work among ourselves.” They do switch the pastries from time to time to have something new. Menu highlights include delivery of coffees to staff and students. Emmanuel, one of the

students who helps run the store said, “Set your heart ablaze.” This sentiment is shared among both the Read an opinion piece on this article on page 13. students and teachers who work together to run the cafe.

articles.body:
Photo by MRL.com By Aracely Rodriguez

After time away from solo releases, Bruno Mars has stepped back into the spotlight with a comeback that feels both nostalgic and refreshing. With new music, a new album, and an exciting tour, his return has sparked conversation among longtime listeners and new fans alike. For many, Bruno Mars’ music has always been a constant presence. Senior Izzy Andrade shared, “Since I could remember as a kid, I’ve been hearing his songs on the radio.” Like many fans, she grew up listening to his hits, including one of her favorites, Just the Way You Are. His earlier songs helped shape pop music in the 2010s and continue to remain popular today. With his comeback, some might wonder whether his new music needs to outdo his older hits to be considered successful. However, Andrade doesn’t believe that’s necessary. “I don’t think he needs to outdo his old music to be successful,” she said. Instead of competing with his past, his new music builds on the strong foundation he already created. His latest songs stay true to the sound that made him popular, smooth vocals, catchy hooks, and a mix of funk and pop influences while still feeling current. Rather than chasing trends, his new album reflects consistency and confidence. It shows that growth doesn’t always mean completely changing direction; sometimes it means refining what already works. Beyond streaming platforms, his comeback is also marked by a new tour. Known for high-energy performances and engaging stage presence, the tour gives fans the chance to experience both classic hits and new tracks live. The combination of nostalgia and fresh material makes the comeback feel complete. Bruno Mars’ return isn’t about replacing his old music. As Andrade’s perspective shows, his success doesn’t depend on surpassing past hits, but on continuing to create music that connects with listeners. With a strong fan base and renewed excitement around his name, this comeback proves that some artists don’t fade — they simply return stronger. In the end, Bruno Mars’ comeback shows that success isn’t always about topping past achievements, but about continuing to connect with audiences. With new music, a new album, and a tour that brings fans together, his return reminds listeners why they supported him in the first place. As senior Izzy Andrade and many others believe, he doesn’t need to outdo his old hits — he simply needs to keep making music that resonates. And judging by the excitement surrounding this new era, his comeback is already doing exactly that.

If you’ve been on TikTok during December and January, you might’ve seen Clash Royale videos with a new, original "song" with satiric lyrics that are sped up and slowed in random intervals. These audios were first created from a user called “andrelikespancakes,” who was one of the first to create these “satire” songs. Over time though, two main users would come to view: Hyperbaiter and Vexy. During all of December 2025, these two users would have somewhat of a "rivalry.” No one really knew who was “better” at the time, since both users created pretty catchy songs. However, on January 3, 2026, Vexy would go on to make a Hyperbaiter Disstrack, and that song would become Vexy best song, gaining millions of views and being used in hundreds of videos. Hyperbaiter, however, would fall off at this point, not uploading many songs while Vexy Disstrack continued to grow in views all across TikTok and Youtube. Since then, Vexy has continued to regularly produce new songs, while Hyperbaiter hasn’t seen much activity, although they stated that the rivalry is still ongoing.. A student who is familiar with the Hyperbaiter and Vexy drama said, “I first came across these audios as Clash Royale videos, and I really liked them at the beginning. It was unique in a time when AI was common, and even Vexy confirming that they use AI to create the song melody and beat, all the lyrics are still human made, so it still felt like a human made it. I mean, the lyrics had mostly human humor, something AI could never top, and I’m glad it stayed that way.” Whether you heard about the beef, glazed at their songs, or don’t know about it at all, this is certainly a unique and interesting style of song creation that still has a bit of human charm to it.

#1. Tennis, Everyone? (A Tribute to the Late, Great James O. Incandenza) by Hakita "Tennis, Everyone? (A Tribute to the Late, Great James O. Incandenza)" is a breakcore and drill and bass music album by the artist Hakita (also known as Heaven Pierce Her.) The album and its tracks are named after fictional movies from the character James O. Incandenza filmography, as detailed in the novel “Infinite Jest" by David Foster Wallace. I love the album for its heavy, complex, and manic percussion, showing Hakita's development and experimentation with different musical ideas.

#2 Pinkerton by Weezer

Pinkerton is Weezer's second studio album. It is the defining "cult classic" of the 90s, famously pivoting from the sunny power-pop of their debut to a raw, abrasive, and deeply personal sound. I love the album because its calming and I grew up listening to this amazing album.

#3 Antics by Interpol Antics is the second studio album by the American post-punk band Interpol. It was the band's major commercial success, reaching #15 on the Billboard 200 and going Gold in the US. I love the album because of the signature post-punk sound with more melodic, accessible hooks.

The protest, sparked by fatal shootings by federal agents in Minneapolis, resulted in business closures and demonstrations in cities like Los Angeles. This was an action across the country where many major cities organized rallies, marches, and direct actions. Americans participated by not going to work or school to join the nationwide shutdown to stop funding ICE and stand against the violence carried out by

The people of Iran are protesting the Iranian government. The protests began on December 28, 2025 by marching on the streets and acts of civil disobedience because of a mix of social, political, and economic reasons. Iran is a theocracy with Shia Islam being its main religion. This is important because of what the protesters want and the very nature of Islam as a religion. The protests in Iran are happening because of severe economic collapse by the 40% inflation, and rising food costs combined with deep anger over political repression. This includes the 2022 killing of Mahsa Amini, and strict Islamic dress codes. Demonstrators and protesters are calling for the end of the Islamic Republic, aiming for a democratic, free, and secular Iran. Public anger is also directed at the government's misallocation of national resources to foreign proxies (such as Hezbollah) while the domestic population faces immense hardship. According to NBC news: Tehran on Friday dismissed as "completely false" Trump’s repeated suggestion that his threats had halted the planned executions of more than 800 protesters. Trump had claimed that his warnings made Iranian authorities cancel mass hangings of detainees, a claim Iran's government and prosecutors rejected outright, calling it baseless propaganda aimed at entering Iran's internal affairs. The U.S. President made the comments aboard Air Force One, stating that "an hour before this horrible event, they canceled it. That was a good sign," while emphasizing the deployment of naval forces as a deterrent. The article highlights the growing tensions, with Trump saying a U.S. naval buildup including an aircraft carrier group and supporting ships as a "massive armada" heading toward the region "just in case" military action becomes necessary. He added, "We have an armada heading that direction and maybe we won’t have to use it. We'll see," framing it as pressure to prevent further bloodshed but leaving open the possibility of intervention if the crackdown intensifies or executions resume. Iran's government provided its first official count, stating 3,117 people had died saying most were civilians and security forces killed by "terrorists," which was a stark contrast to independent estimates and underscoring the regime's narrative of external sabotage rather than domestic dissent. In short, these protests represent many of the issues to Iran’s theocratic rule since 1979, driven by economic disaster and the people’s calls for the country’s regime change, but met with lethal repression that has quelled momentum while exposing deep internal fractures and drawing international scrutiny.

articles.title: Welcome to Fashion Week 2026
articles.author: Alma Santiago and Samantha Martinez
articles.dek: The Railsplitter
articles.body:
Fashion Week 2026 has been bringing creativity and a peek at the future style across the “Big Four” which are New York, London, Milan, and Paris. Fashion designers from these four cities have shown that fashion is no longer just about looks, but about its innovation and identity. Big brands like Prada and Louis Vuitton mixed modern tech with classic designs. For example, fabrics that change color with the temperature and with the use of technology. This feature makes their clothing adaptable to its wearer. Beneath the dazzling lights and the modern aesthetics, many of the designers showcased their roots, demonstrating that fashion is part of a vibrant identity. With all of these amazing clothes, nearly every collection used an eco-friendly material like some using recycled silk and ocean plastic. Designer Stella McCartney did not use animal products in her fashion to help the environment. Since she started her designer brand in 2001 she has never used any animal products at all. Overall, Fashion Week 2026 showed that future fashion is bright. It reminds people that creativity can shape a better world when combined with style and care for the environment. Fashion Week will continue in Paris from March 2-10 and the public is excited to see what Paris has in store for them.

Lunar New Year is a holiday celebrated by millions of people from all over the world. It marks the beginning of a new year that is based on the Lunar calendar which usually falls between late January and mid-February. This year, Lunar New Year begins on February 17, 2026, and it's the year of the horse. The holiday is widely celebrated in China, Vietnam, Korea, and other Asian countries. Although traditions are different in each culture, the main purpose of this holiday is to spend time with family and welcome good luck for the upcoming year. Many families gather around for a special dinner on New Year's Eve. Certain foods have a special meaning. For example, dumplings represent wealth and fish symbolizes good fortune. Red decorations are common because the color of red is believed to bring luck and happiness. Children also often receive red envelopes that are filled with money as a symbol of good wishes. Jack Chen, a student from Lincoln High School said, “During Chinese New Year, my family and I have a big family dinner which usually consists of crab, boiled chicken, and a bunch of other different vegetables and meats. To prepare for the new year I cut my hair and deep cleaned my room.” David Lin another student from Lincoln High School said, “We have a big meal, pray to ancestors, and visit family” Overall, Lunar New Year is a time to celebrate family, culture, and new beginnings.

Looking for volunteer hours? Want to help your community? 24 LA is an amazing nonprofit company committed to helping families and communities that have lost their belongings in the California fires. This company is always looking for volunteers who have a passion for helping people. They are also committed to helping the community by creating thousands of fundraisers and clothing drives and during the holidays giving back to families without a Christmas. The creator of this amazing organization, Kenyan Armitage, is committed to making a difference and helping people in these hard times. An inspiring quote from her is “I knew someone who was directly affected by the California fires and it broke me to know that there was no way for me to help more people during these terrible times. So I started my non-profit with my best friend and it's an amazing feeling to know you are a part of something bigger than yourself. We are always looking for caring volunteers who want to help their community.” So those looking for volunteer hours, this might be the company for you! For more information visit: @24laproject

Research shows that when students listen to music it “can improve and promote a state of calm focus’(Levine Music). Not only this, but music is heavily connected to memory. Music affects areas of the brain such as the hippocampus and frontal cortex. These areas influence the section of the brain associated with memory and emotion. The music that a student could have been listening to when studying, may spark a recollection when replayed another time. This is important for students because it can help them remember the material better. Ms. Sierra believes that listening to music in school may be a distraction to students; however she concludes that it's a good reward for students that have already completed their assignments. However, an article from the National Library of Medicine states that “music with lyrics is generally detrimental to cognitive performance...” Cognitive performance is the method the brain uses to efficiently complete mental tasks, requiring memory or attention. Students clearly function cognitive thinking as the work to finish a task. Nevertheless, it may be true that music is disruptive to the cognitive thinking process, but there's more evidence that music provides positive benefits rather than negative. According to Healthline, the best type will depend on the individual. There is evidence that classical symphonies or relaxing music are good choices for managing stress, but upbeat music might also boost a person’s thinking processes. Instrumental music may be more suitable than songs with lyrics, as the lyrics can be distracting. Ultimately, the restriction of blocking Spotify on chromebooks may or may not hinder students ability to study. However, it may cause more disruptive issues with phone use. Students who want to enjoy music, but have no access to the website on the chromebooks may pull out cellphones against the school's policy.

articles.title: Search for Gold
articles.author: Adrian Espinoza
articles.dek: Peach Bowl Loss to No. 1 Indiana By Ruby Campos
articles.body:
Winter Olympics 2026 Highlights By Elsa Daily

On January 9, 2026 the Chick-fil-A Peach Bowl hosted one of the most exciting games of the college football season. Held at Mercedes Benz Stadium in Atlanta, Georgia, The Peach Bowl serves as a college football playoff semifinal, giving the winning team a chance to advance to the national championship This year’s game featured the Indiana Hoosiers and the Oregon Ducks. Indiana entered the game as the No.1 ranked team while the Oregon ducks ranked No. 5 in the College Football Playoff ranking. The game was full of action and impressive plays. Indian’s quarterback, Fernando Mendoza, led the offense with strong passing and smart decisions while the defense led by cornerback D’Angelo Ponds made several crucial plays which stopped Oregon’s drives that helped Indiana maintain control of the game. Oregon also showcased its talent with several big plays and fast paced offense but Indiana ultimately came out on top. The 2026 Chick-fil-A Peach Bowl, concluded with No. 1 Indiana defeating No. 5 Oregon 56-22.

The 2026 Winter Olympics have come and gone, and some moments are still worth talking about. Here’s The Railsplitters round-up of some of the most talked about and anticipated events.

lia Malinin “Quad Gods” Too Close To The Sun The hype around U.S. figure skater Ilia Malinin has been through the roof, and rightfully so. Malinin, at only 21, has achieved moments in his career other athletes could only dream of. At 17, he became the only person in history to ever successfully land a quad axel jump —the hardest figure skating jump— in competition. He’s gone on to complete this jump a numerous amount of times after this, earning him the nickname “Quad God.” His success made him an obvious favorite for the individual men’s figure skating gold. Everything seemed as if it was coming to fruition, like fate. The problem is, the media was already naming him as “one of the greatest to ever do it.” All before he had ever even competed in a single Olympic games. The Google commercials and news anchor coverage were acting as if he had already won Olympic gold. The pressure was high, and Malinin felt it. The first crack in the ice came in the team event. Audiences were expecting his signature quad axel in both the short program, and free skate. What viewers didn’t know is that he had never even planned to attempt it in these programs. Nevertheless, the online criticism came crashing through the floodgates. This added on to the hype already surrounding the young athlete, and when he failed to perform to those standards, he was ridiculed. Nevertheless, it wasn’t a complete disaster. Although not to his full potential, Malini's contribution to the team event was just what Team USA needed to clinch the gold. It’s clear he’s learned a lot this Olympic cycle, and not going home empty handed will soften that blow.

The Los Angeles Rams lost in the end against the Seattle Seahawks even with a 31-27 lead. In the first quarter Walker made the first touchdown, helping lead the Seahawks 7-0. Mevis made a field goal putting up points for the Rams and a 7-3 score. Then in the last few seconds Myers made a field goal making it 10-3. Going into the 2nd quarter Mevis made another field goal making the score 10-6. On the 1:55 minute mark Williams made a touchdown and Mevis with the extra point to help the Rams to gain the lead 10-13. Then in the last 20 seconds of the 2nd quarter Smith made a touchdown and Myers made the extra point and the Seahawks led the game again 17-13. Going into the 3rd quarter J.Bobo made a touchdown and Myers made another extra point and the score was 24-13 Seattle. With 9:41 to go, Adams made a touchdown for the Rams and Mevis got an extra point making it 24-20. Kupp made a touchdown and Myers yet again made that extra point. In the last 2 minutes in the 3rd quarter Stafford passed to Nacua making a touchdown. Officials reviewed the pass and the pass was good. In the last quarter the Seattle Seahawks had amazing defence even when they were closer to their touchdown. Matthew Stafford had missed many opportunities to run and make a touchdown in the last couple of minutes but at last the Seattle Seahawks won the game and went to Super Bow] 60 going against the New England Patriots. Defensive tackle Kobie Turner said, “It’s all of these moments that just make me so grateful. This stings, it really does,” Turner said. “These scars continue to heal us and I can’t wait to be able to come back next year and to continue to build on this.”

Kazakhstan Wins First Olympic Gold Figure Skating Medal Malini's fall from grace proved to be a silver lining. Kazakhstan’s Mikhail Sidorov delivered an impeccable free skate routine, securing him the gold for the first time in his country’s history. Both Japan’s Yuma Kagiyama and Sato Shun were serious contenders for the spot. Those two, along with Malinin, were the obvious podium contenders. Then Shaidorov came along. It’s not like Mikhail Sidorov is a huge shock. Throughout his career, he’s racked up an impressive resume. A silver medal in the 2025 World Cup and a four-time Grand Prix medalist top the list. For his free skate program, Sidorov skated to a mashup of Adriano Celentano Confessa and the Diva Dance from the 1997 sci-fi film The Fifth Element. He landed five quadruple jumps throughout the routine, leading him to earn his highest ever free-skate score, a whopping 291.58 points. This is a historic win for Kazakhstan, and their first figure skating Olympic gold. Sidorov, at age 21, has cemented himself in figure skating history for good.

Ml)

The winter olympics are finally over, so let's do a summary of Team USA. Team USA collected 12 gold medals. Team USA Hockey made the biggest sweep getting gold in mens and womens and both defeating Canada twice in overtime. If you did the math in men's hockey the average was 4.33 goals per game and in all they made 26 goals. Going to women's hockey, their average was 4.71 which was better than men's hockey. They scored 33 and only let 2 goals against them. Women dominated this 2026 winter olympics winning 8 of the 12 golds. In the individual group 5 women got gold for Team USA. Breezy Johnson secured gold in women's downhill getting Team USA their first gold for the 2026 winter olympic. Mikaela Shiffrin won the women's slalom and became the first U.S. skier to win three career Olympic golds. Alysa Liu won gold in women's single figure skating ending a 24 year drought for American women. In Womens monobob (bobsled) Elana Meyers Taylor won gold and became the oldest American to win an individual winter olympic gold. She is 41 years old. Elizabeth Lemley won gold in women's moguls (freestyle skiing) in her first Olympic debut and led a 1-2 finish for Team USA. In the women's team and mixed gold they got the last three medals. U.S. women's ice hockey defeated Canada 2-1 in overtime reclaiming gold once again and being led by Hilary Knight and Megan Keller. In Figure Skating, Team USA’s Alysa Liu, Amber Glenn, Madison Chock, and Ellie Kam and a few male teammates won gold and defended their gold title. And the last team was Mixed aerials. Kaila Kuhn, Christopher Lillis, and Connor Curran secured gold in freestyle skiing. Moving on to the Men's team, they won the remaining 4 gold medals for team USA. In men's hockey they also won 2-1 against Canada in overtime with Jack Hughes scoring the golden shot helping the U.S. get gold for the first time since 1980. In Speed Skating Jordan Stolz won two individual gold medals. He had set records in both 500m and 1,000m becoming the first American also since 1980 to win multiple speed skating gold. Coming next is Alex Ferreira won gold in men's freeski halfpipe. He had completed his career Olympic medals collection (gold solver, and bronze) and also became the oldest halfpipe gold medal winner at the age of 31.

Olympians Alysa Liu, Mikaela Shiffrin, and Erin Jackson Photo by Getty Images

Brazil’s Lucas Pinheiro Braathen Wins South America’s First Winter Olympic Medal The Winter Olympics are notoriously dominated by countries with colder climates. A sun-drenched nation like Brazil doesn’t seem like it would fare well in disciplines competed at the winter games. Nevertheless, these games are always full of surprises. A Norwegian born skier to a Brazilian mother spent his childhood in between Brazil and Norway. He started skiing at around 4 years old, and has made a name for himself doing so. Braathens track record in the Olympics hasn’t been strong. Over his two Olympics, he’s DNFd at a total of 3 out of his 4 events. On his 4th ever Olympic event however, he managed to win Gold. This is a landmark achievement for both Brazil and South America. He skied into first place with a total time of 2:25.00, placing ahead of Switzerland’s Marco Odermatt - the defending champion- and Loic Meillard. This was a historic run, and a first Winter Olympic medal for both Brazil and South America. Accomplishments from underrepresented countries like this and the Jamaican Bobsled team are a joy to see at these games.

Mathilde Gremaud Defends Slopestyle Gold Against China’s Superstar, Eileen Gu Women’s Slopestyle was one of the most anticipated events of the 2026 games. Previous Olympic champion Eileen Gu was a favorite to win gold, her only gold medal left to get. However, defending Olympic champion Mathilde Gremaud would not go without a fight. Eileen Gu, at age 22, is the most decorated female freeskier in history and the first athlete to medal in three freeski events during a single Olympic games. Gremaud accomplishments are sizable as well. She’s medaled in all 3 of her Olympic games, became the first woman to land a switch double cork 1440 in competition history. Gremaud won with a score of 86.96, just 0.38 points higher than Gu’s 86.58. Gremaud locked in this score on her second run down, and was unbeatable throughout the rest of the competition. The battle for gold was tight amongst all competitors but Mathilde Gremaud continues her reign as the queen of Slopestyle.

The Railsplitter

articles.dek: Rebecca Lee Crumpler (1831-1895)
articles.body:
Black history is a foundational, 400-year-plus narrative detailing the experiences, struggles, and profound contributions of people of African descent to world, American, and Canadian society. It spans from the 1619 arrival of enslaved Africans to the ongoing fight for civil rights, highlighting resilience, cultural impact, and achievements in arts, science, and politics.

By Jonathan Mendoza In 1864, Rebecca Lee Crumpler became the first Black Woman in America to get a medical degree After finishing her studies she moved to Virginia to help formerly enslaved people to give them the healthcare and treatment they deserve. She was a hard worker and worked with doctors even though they didn't treat her well due to her gender and color. This difficult experience gave her more confidence, energy and passion into her medical work and encouraged her to do better. In the end she moved back to Boston where she spent her time caring for sick children.

Gordon Parks (1912-2006) By Audrie Ozuna Gordon Parks was a famous African American Filmmaker, photographer, and writer. He was born November 30th 1912 and unfortunately died March 7th 2006. He became the first Black photographer for Life magazine, where his work powerfully documented the everyday lives and struggles of Black Americans. Parks also broke barriers in film as the first Black director of a major Hollywood studio film, The Learning Tree His photography and films centered humanity, dignity and resilience at a time when Black voices were often excluded.

Aretha Franklin (1942-2018)

By Adrian Espinoza Aretha Franklin “The Queen of Soul” a famous African American singer born March 25, 1942. She was an American Singer, songwriter, and a pianist. She had the most rich, powerful voice, mostly singing Soul and R&B. Started her career in her early teens and signed with Columbia in 1960. By the 1970’s she became a legend even the rolling stone magazine called her the greatest singer of all time, she passed away in August 16, 2018.

Photo by History.com

Arthur Ashe (1943-1993) By Jada Guevara

Arthur Ashe a famous American tennis player was born on July 10, 1943 and sadly died February 6. 1993. He was the first Black player selected to the United States Davis Cup team. Where people get picked to play in a team to compete in “World Cup Tennis”. Arthur Ashe believed in no such thing of segregation. By this he demonstrates by winning multiple titles like Wimbledon (1975) , US open (1968), and Australian open (1970). Later, Ashe became an author, educator, and a campaigner for civil rights. One of Arther Ashe’s quotes is “Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.” Arthur Ashe received many rewards and honor for what he has done throughout his lifetime. During this month we especially recognize what he has done.

ournalist

Ida B. Wells (1862-1931) By Samantha Martinez

Ida B. Wells was a Journalist born on July 16th 1862 and died at the age of 69 on March 25th 1931. Ida was born into slavery in Mississippi less than a year before the Emancipation Proclamation. When she was about 20 she moved to Tenissie and started writing for black newspapers. She would write about segregated schools that would force children of color to go to different schools, and forms of discrimination in southern schools. One of her friends got killed and it caused Wells to start writing differently. She went around interviewing people about the truth and published the articles in a Tennessee Newspaper. Since the articles were about what was truly happening people were very angry and she had to leave the South forever. She continued writing when she was living in New York and Chicago. On March 25th 1931 she died due to a kidney disease. Although she is no longer with us she still inspires people to fight for change.

f. “a! Photo by History.com

Harriet Tubman was born into slavery in March 1822 and died March 10, 1913. She was an underground railroad conductor, civil war spy, and she escaped slavery in 1849. One of her accomplishments was freeing approximately 70 African American slaves in the north. Harriet was the first woman to lead an operation in the U.S. military when she planned to lead an armed raid which was successful. After the war, she lived in Auburn in New York, where she supported former enslaved people, worked for women's voting rights and remained a powerful symbol of resistance until her death.

articles.body:
Photo by Reddit

Everyone has heard of the Epstein files at some point. Many are aware of them from the internet, news reports or word of mouth. The subject has overwhelmed the daily newscycle for the last two years. One recent event involves the disruption to school picture plans after online posts linked Lifetouch to Epstein. Lifetouch is a major, 90-year-old national photography company specializing in school portraits (preschool through graduation), sports, and yearbooks. Owned by Shutterfly, it operates across North America and manages "Picture Day" for over 50,000 schools. According to news reports, Lifetouch was first established by investment fund manager Apollo Global Management. Apollo’s former CEO is billionaire investor Leon Black, who met regularly with Epstein and was advised by Epstein on financial matters. No one knew why he was in the files initially, but later reports revealed that Black had connections to Epstein. According to The Associated Press, he had multiple meetings with Epstein and was mentioned thousands of times in the files. In January 2021, Apollo announced Black would step down as CEO by his 70th birthday (July 2021) but remain as chairman. On March 22, 2021, it was announced he was leaving his roles as CEO and Chairman immediately to focus on family and health, following an independent review of his financial relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. The investigation revealed Black paid Epstein $158 million between 2012 and 2017 for tax and estate planning, which triggered investor concern. Lifetouch is one of the most well known companies when it comes to school photos with 25 million students and 50,000 schools using Lifetouch for their school picture days, ID photos, and yearbook portraits. Now, with the exposure of the CEO, many schools are cancelling their school picture days (mainly ones that use Lifetouch). The company itself fought back when the new Lifetouch Group CEO Ken Murphy issued a statement addressing what the company describes as misinformation regarding the alleged sharing of school files. Lifetouch has assured school districts that no student photographs have been distributed to third parties.with the new Murphy himself sending out a public statement that,’Lifetouch is not named in the Epstein files. The documents contain no allegations that Lifetouch itself was involved in, or that student photos were used in, any illicit activities.” Only the original CEO Leon Black was mentioned in the files and the company Lifetouch itself was never mentioned but many school districts either canceled their school picture days entirely or cut all relations with Lifetouch.

articles.title: GRAMMYS VS TRUMP
articles.author: Jonathan Mendoza
articles.dek: How music's biggest stars protested Trump's
articles.body:
immigration crackdown at the Grammys. By Jada Guvera

On February 1, 2026 the Grammys was held in LA at the crypto arena. The artists spoke out against President Trump to show their support to the community and the growing cultural revolt against immigration crackdowns. Some of the artists were Bad Bunny, Billie Eilish, and other artists voicing their stand against ICE and the Trump administration. The Grammy’s is an event where all the artists come to receive one of the highest and most prestigious honors presented by The Recording Academy. It is recognized as “music's biggest night.” During this seven hour long ceremony, artists supported the community through their acceptance speeches. Here is a list of some of the most notable quotes: Bad Bunny, when winning the Album of the Year award, said during his acceptance speech “ICE out! We’re not savages. We’re not animals. We are not aliens. We are humans, and we are Americans. He emphasized that “the only thing that is more powerful than hate is love,” while he was urging people “ to fight with love, not hate.” Bad bunny dedicated his

Photo by Music.com

On February 8, 2026, Bad Bunny made history at the Super Bowl. As a global superstar from Puerto Rico, he became the first artist to perform a halftime show almost entirely in Spanish. Although he brought out major guests like Lady Gaga and Karol G, the performance sparked significant debate. Due to the language barrier, some fans felt the show didn't resonate as strongly as past performances. TikTok videos captured stadium crowds that didn't seem as enthusiastic as the viewers watching from home. For many, the set felt like a tribute specifically for the Latino and immigrant communities, especially given the current political climate regarding ICE. Even if the stadium audience wasn't entirely engaged, the performance represented a massive leap forward for Spanish-speaking artists on the world's biggest stage. In an interview with Time reporters, Richard Correll stated: “I really like its positive message; it wasn't specific to one culture, but for everyone, and I especially like that. No one really knew what he was saying, but the message still got across. I was most impressed with the visuals—specifically when they put grass on the field to represent different countries.” While Bad Bunny’s performance might not have been a major hit for everyone in the stadium, it was a triumph for immigrants and many others. This halftime show was more than just a performance; it was a major step in showing that everyone is equal, regardless of race, gender, or background.

Photo by Grammy.com

award, “to the people who left their homeland to pursue their dreams.” Billie Eilish won the “Song of the Year award” and during her acceptance speech she said, “No one is illegal on stolen land” to emphasize her support for the community and speak for justice. Olivia Dean won the “Best New Artists award” and during her acceptance speech she said, “I guess I wanna say that I am up here as a granddaughter of an immigrant. I am a product of bravery and I think those people should be celebrated. We are nothing without each other.” Kehlani won the “Best R&B Performance.” One point stood out when she said, “Everybody is so powerful in this room and in this room later. And together we’re stronger in numbers to speak against all the injustice going on in the world right now. So instead of letting it be just a couple few here and there, I hope everybody’s inspired to join together as a community of artists. Speak out against what’s going on.” These artists stood up and used their voice to communicate important messages. With this support to the community, it helps others see how they should use their voice for what's right. Think about how you can use your voice when standing up for justice. If you need help, look at these artists who spoke their thoughts knowing the risks of losing fans, but still made a difference with their outspokenness for what's right.

articles.title: Tigerchella Talent Abounds at This Year’s Tigerchella Festival
articles.author: Eliana Gabriel
articles.dek: The Railsplitter
articles.body: On January 30th, in the light of early afternoon, hundreds of students were ushered out the front doors of the 200 building, while performers made final checks on their equipment, or rehearsed their performances. A makeshift stage composed of metal pallets and thick, heavy black drapes sat in the free speech area, assembled by Mr. Gladden and a few other helpful theater students the previous day. As freshmen and seniors alike filled out the tables, benches, sat on the ground, or even stood from a distance, all eyes were set on the stage, ready for one of the biggest school events of the year. Tigerchella is an annual talent show hosted by the ASB, or Associated Student Body, of Abraham Lincoln Senior High school, every spring semester. Though the exact timeframe and locale changes, alternating between an indoor or outdoor locale and at one point occurring as late as May, its purpose always remains the same; to boost morale for the school and promote community. Though this year’s event was wrought with several issues, like speakers shorting out or equipment malfunctioning, the energy kept going strong. People swapped performance order whenever instrumental soundtracks abruptly stopped on their own, maintaining crowd interest while still giving performers and technicians alike the time they needed for some maintenance checks. The majority of the events lined up that day involved singing in various genres, though there were a few outliers, such as Mr. Brewer’s comedy performance, or Motion Heights’ dance routine. Christine, one performer at the event, had her performance of “From the start”, by Laufey, dedicated to her crush. “I was nervous,” she says, “but I wanted him to know, and for him to read this article and know.” This event was a beautiful showing of school spirit and creative talent, like it’s always been, and I’m sure students and teachers alike can’t wait to see who steps onstage next year.

articles.title: Educare: Not Just an Afterschool Program
articles.author: The Railsplitter

articles.title: More Than Makeup: Inside Lincoln's Glam Club
articles.author: Aracely Rodriguez
articles.body:
By Megan Nim Looking for a place to hangout, have fun, and learn new skills after school? Educare is a nonprofit organization that gives students a safe, welcoming place after school to learn, have fun, and explore new interests. For the past 10 years, the organization has offered academic support, sports, and enrichment activities. The program runs from 3:24pm to 6:00pm and is free to all students. Students can join a variety of clubs and programs, including fitness and sports (body conditioning, fitness center, badminton, dance), academic clubs (math clubs, Academic Decathlon), creative clubs (Cooking club, DIY club, music club, the glam room), and hobby clubs (Compassion in Action, Theater club, and International student club). The Educare staff are planning to add programs such as a DJ club and Graffiti art next semester. Christopher Garcia, an 11th grade student who is a part of the cooking club said, “I love spending time cooking with my friends.” Helen Flores, a 11th grade student who is a part of the math club said, “I enjoy studying with my friends for class or practicing for math competitions. The club is pretty fun and welcoming to all students in any math since they have a lot of tutors ready to help anyone.” Joining Educare lets students try new activities, make friends, and enjoy a space where they can truly be themselves.

At Lincoln High School, students interested in beauty and cosmetology have found a creative outlet through the school’s Glam Club. Founded by Educare, the club provides a welcoming space for students to explore the beauty industry while developing practical skills and self-confidence. Through hands-on activities, mentorship, and access to real materials, members are able to learn techniques that go beyond what is typically offered in a regular classroom. Glam Club focuses on learning through experience. Members create their own lip glosses, design and apply nails, and practice makeup techniques on silicone practice faces. The materials used during meetings are provided by Educare staff, allowing students to experiment without worrying about the cost of supplies. In addition, students receive personal makeup items such as eyeshadow, lip liners, and lipsticks, giving them the opportunity to practice and refine their skills. Students say the club offers more than just beauty training. It provides a place to be imaginative. One member explained, “I joined the Glam Club because I really wanted to have a place to express myself more.” Activities such as learning nail techniques, creating lip gloss, and practicing makeup allow members to explore their creativity in a supportive environment.For senior Mia, the club also serves as a social space. She shared that she joined to fill her free time and learn more about makeup. “I spent time with my friends,and I experimented with makeup with mannequin faces, and was able to create more friendships,” she said. Her experience highlights how the club helps students connect with others who share similar interests. Members also believe Glam Club has made a positive impact on the school community. According to one student, the club has encouraged students to be more expressive and open about who they are. Another student noted that the club helps “students express themselves and make relationships out of makeup and girlhood,” emphasizing the sense of belonging it creates.Beyond teaching technical skills, Glam Club introduces students to potential career paths in cosmetology and the beauty industry. It also provides a constructive after-school activity where students can build confidence, relieve stress, and develop teamwork skills. The guidance from Educare staff ensures that students learn in a structured and encouraging environment. Overall, Glam Club is more than just a place to practice makeup ,it is a community where students can learn, create, and grow together. Through hands-on experience, supportive mentorship from Educare, and opportunities for self-expression, the club empowers students at Lincoln High School to build confidence and explore their passions for beauty and cosmetology.

articles.author: The Railsplitter
articles.body: Photo by Aracely Rodriguez .

articles.title: S.P.A.R.K Club
articles.author: Jada Guevara Photo by Tiffany Nguyen
articles.dek: Students Paving A Road to Knowledge
articles.body:
On March 13th, to celebrate Pi Day, MST planned a competition to see who could count the most digits in the infinite number of Pi. The winner, who counted up to 153 digits was Jiayu Li. She is a ninth grader and participated on behalf of Ms. Sara’s advisory class. She was proud to be the Pi winner, but felt she could have recited more and continued to reach a higher goal. She said, “I originally remembered 400 digits, but was stopped by the judges due to a misconception of getting a digit wrong.” She prepared in many ways and one thing she mentioned was that she prepared by using a song focused on Pi. She chose to do this competition because before high school she was the president of her middle school. She participated in her school competitions and wanted to give it a try at Lincoln. Previously she got to 150 digits and this year she passed her record by 3 digits. She hopes she will do better and go higher next year. After this event, and during lunch, the teachers and ASB set up a “pie in the face" event where many teachers participated and all the students had fun. Pi is the special number when you divide any circle’s circumference (distance around a circle) by its diameter (distance across). This number is infinite, but what makes it special is that it doesn’t repeat. Pi is an infinite number, up till this day math solvers are trying to find the concluding digit of pi. Pi is crucial in everyday lives. For instance, engineering, architecture, technology, space exploration, and more use Pi. So, if you want to participate next year, just look at a circle and count 3.1415926....

Photo by Katherine Jimenez

Your future is a highway, but knowledge is the pavement. Start building your road today with S.P.A.R.K! The S.P.A.R.K (Students Paving A Road to Knowledge) Club is a great opportunity to grow and receive guidance throughout your years of high school. You get to meet new people and gain social and leadership skills. This club meets two times a semester in room 152 and holds fun events. The S.P.A.R.K Club, run by Ms. Kristensen and Gabrielle Hernandez, is a mentoring program for students here at Lincoln. Seniors in the club act as “big buddies” or mentors to lowerclassmen and juniors. This intent of the club is to guide students through college admissions and help them succeed in their years leading up to the application season. Seniors benefit too, as they receive community service hours for their time in the club. The Railsplitter interviewed Junior, Valerie Sanchez who said, "It's a good club to join for students who need help with college related things.” Gabrielle Hernandez with Valerie Sanchez planned on starting this club for this school year because they wanted students to have a place where they can look for help to make high school feel easier when communicating with their teachers and other students so they are not alone throughout their path of success to thrive in school. The S.P.A.R.K Club wants you to be part of their community, supportive, and uplifting team!

articles.title: Pet Peeves At School
articles.author: Yew Yu
articles.dek: Xbox To be “Sunsetted” After 24 Years
articles.body:
By Samantha Ortiz A pet peeve refers to an annoying behavior or action that one finds irritating more than others. Some believe that the root of a pet peeve grows out of a violation of social expectations. According to psychology today, there is a “possibility that what started as a small minor annoyance can stem into a major frustration.” School can be an expansion ground for pet peeves on account of the fact that people follow different behaviors that others may not be pleased with. A student expressed that their biggest pet peeve in school is when students walk at a slow pace in the hall. They further revealed that this irritated them because such behavior “blocks their path to class” and sometimes it becomes the reason for why they take so long to get to class. Based on a survey conducted, a certain percentage of students agreed with this behavior as well. Students also shared that the “no cellphone policy” has become rather annoying as well. One student in particular found it bothersome how administration “freaks out” about phones being out in class, especially when there are more important issues to detail with on a daily basis. These devices are then taken away from students which others argue now cannot be used in case of emergencies.

When it comes to gaming, Microsoft is widely considered to be one of the most well known gaming console companies in the world, having produced multiple generational consoles like the Xbox and its other generations, while also being somewhat relevant in modern day gaming, although its appeal was slowly fading toward the mid 2020s. When a new CEO for Microsoft Gaming (Asha Sharma) was announced in February 2026, nobody really thought too much of it- until the new CEO announced their subjective decision to “sunset” Xbox to focus heavily on AI. Sunsetting in this context basically means that Microsoft will gradually transition away from Xbox to work on AI. However, some people are not happy with this change, with some saying on TikTok that this decision is just another cash grab. Others are sad about this decision, saying that this will be the “end of an era’, as most people (usually young adults) on the Internet grew up playing Xbox games in their teenage years.

In the end, pet peeves reveal how small annoyances can reflect deeper social expectations, concerns and frustrations within a school environment. Recognizing these complexities can help create a more empathetic and effective school culture.

@ Console Gaming (Xbox, Playstation) Mobile Gaming PC Gaming

Student Responses to School Pet Peeves

@ Handheld Gaming (Nintendo Switch, Steam Deck)

Hallway Congestion and Slow Movement: The most frequently cited pet peeve is students being slow walkers, often specifically mentioned in hallways, sometimes combined with groups forming "walls" and taking up all the space. Restrictive Technology/Music Policies: Many respondents dislike the no-phone policy (which some feel is treating students like children), the inability to use AirPods or listen to music, and Spotify being blocked. Restroom Issues: A significant concern is closed restrooms or locked restrooms, as well as students misusing the facilities by vaping or just hanging out instead of using the stalls for their intended purpose. Disruptive Student Behavior: Other major annoyances include chewing gum out loud, constant complaining, excessive public displays of affection (PDA) by couples in hallways, and students causing unnecessary distractions or lacking manners.

This graph perfectly represents the slow “decline” of popularity in terms of Xbox gaming nowadays, as only around ~31% of Lincoln students use console gaming, while the mass majority games on PCs. One Lincoln student even said,” I used to be excited in 2020 to play my PSS after a long day of online classes. Now, I barely even play my consoles because PC gaming is so much more dominant.” Another student stated,” Yeah, I remember the hype around the PS5 when it first came out. Now, I just have either my phone or my PC. Console gaming is definitely dying.” Even though gaming is now more popular than ever, consoles are starting to die out as they are replaced by more “modern” gaming solutions, marking the end of an era where students would be excited to hop on their consoles to game with friends, and Xbox being now “sunsetted” is only the start of the decline of console gaming.

articles.title: Southern California Heat Wave: Record-Breaking March Temperatures
articles.author: Samantha Martinez and Alma Santiago
articles.dek: @ Severe impact, greatly disrupts my life
articles.body:
By Qimei Li Los Angeles experienced an “anomalously early" heat wave causing high temperatures in mid-March. According to the National Weather Service, in the LA city area, the highest temperature from March 17th to March 20th was on or above 95 degrees Fahrenheit. The highest temperature was 98 degrees Fahrenheit on Tuesday, March 17th. On March 20, some parts of California reached 112°F, breaking the all-time U.S. record for the highest March temperature 108°F. The heatwave was caused by a strong high-pressure system in the atmosphere. The heat dome trapped warm air over the city; this system blocked the cool air from the Pacific Ocean and combined with the direct sunlight temperature kept rising. To prevent students from getting heat stroke in the hot weather, Lincoln opened the auditorium during lunchtime for students. Air conditioning was turned on in the auditorium to cool the student down, but some students still preferred to have lunch outside in the shade. A student Wenjing Zhong said, “Even in the shade I still feel incredibly hot. My iPad is also going to burn up.” The survey showed 56.8% of Lincoln students thought the heatwave had a severe impact that disrupted their life. A student commented, “It affects me to such a great degree that I feel like a completely different person. If the Hulk was real, I believe I would be the embodiment before he became mature in his form and was full of pure rage.” By the second week, the temperature had dropped to 90°F. Rising temperatures absorb moisture from the soil and plants, exacerbating the California drought. A climate scientist Swain predicts in the coming months, CA will face more extreme weather. Climate change will cause temperatures to rise even higher. When temperatures are this hot, it is recommended to stay hydrated, stay in cool places, avoid peak sun and limit outdoor activities.

Cesar Chavez, longtime farmworker activist, was recently accused of sexual misconduct. So far five women have come forward accusing him of sexual assault, abuse, and rape when they were young adults and children during the ongoing civil rights campaigns in the 1960s . Over the past 40 years, Cesar Chavez has been integrated into American education and history, and his name has become synonymous with schools, streets, and other public infrastructure due to his radical efforts for American farmworkers and his movement to fight for the rights of migrant workers. Although these contributions have caused important change, his behavior and actions are now being questioned due to new information regarding his own personal history. A victim that has spoken out is Debra Huerta, a Co—Founder of the UFW alongside Chavez. While they were working together Chavez sexually assaulted Huerta twice and it resulted in pregnancies. Ms. Huerta stated, “Unfortunately, he used some of his great leadership to abuse women and children — it’s really awful.” According to the New York Times, he also “assaulted two young girls at the ages of 12 and 13.”

News about this has recently been uncovered. His victims finally came out nearly 50 years later because of a woman named Ana Murguia. She stated that a street near where she lives was in the process of being changed to Cesar Chavez Boulevard. Her experience was so traumatizing that she tried to end her life multiple times by the age of 15 which was stated in the The New York Times. Recently, some people have been arguing about the fact that we should not celebrate Caesar Chavez's birthday. This is due to the fact that there are a lot of allegations about him assaulting women. According to PBS.org, The California State University San Marcos took years to establish a plaza with a statue in his honor, but once the allegations came out about him it only took a matter of days to cover and remove the statue. One political science professor stated, "Our symbols should align with our values. And sadly, the symbol of the Chavez statue, and all of the shame that comes with it now, the reality is it no longer aligns with our values." One of our Lincoln teachers said, “I am very disappointed to hear about the disturbing news. I am saddened to hear about the sufferings of the victims. It's important that our society supports all victims. I am proud of the Chicano community for acting quickly to remove his name and image from tributes and I hope all communities learn from this example."

On the night of February Ist, 2024, 84 year old Nancy Guthrie, mother of Savannah Guthrie, was abducted from her home in Arizona at 1:45am. Savannah Guthrie, best known for her TV segment on the Today Show, has spent the last 44 days not knowing what happened to her mother. The night in question began with a fun evening spent with her family and friends. Nancy came home and headed to bed when in the middle of the night she was abducted. FBI agents say that her alarm system was disarmed at 1:47am along with her pacemaker app being disabled at 2:28 am. This strange and abrupt abduction has left the family and internet at a loss and wondering why someone would do this? This disappearance has been spiraling all over the internet contributing to conspiracy theories as to why this happened. One conspiracy involves events that happened prior to the abduction and if it has something to do with her daughter Savannah? It has been over 44 days and there is still no sign of life. The family continues to stay hopeful that their mother will be returned. Authorities are warning of multiple scams attempting to exploit the high-profile, ongoing investigation into the disappearance. As of late March 2026, the Pima County Sheriff’s Department (PCSD) has warned of dozens of fake fundraising accounts (such as on GoFundMe) attempting to raise money, with some falsely claiming to support billboard campaigns for the case. PCSD emphasizes there are no official fundraisers or GoFundMe efforts associated with the Guthrie investigation. In February 2026, federal investigators reported a rise in "imposter" ransom demands. Specifically, a California man was accused of sending fake ransom demands for Bitcoin to the family. The FBI and local police have so far no leads on the case and the question why and where is still unknown. In these cases the questions always come down to where, when and why. The “why” is still yet to be determined.

Every bracelet purchased helps support local artisans in Bali and Guatemala by providing the reliable source of income they need right now while creating new opportunities for growth, health, education, equality, and prosperity in the future.

articles.title: Fake News: Can You Tell the Difference?
articles.author: Jennifer Month - Hernandez
articles.body:
Today it is hard to discern real information from fake news. Fake news can lead to chaos and confusion. The ability to distinguish real information from fake news has become crucial. The rapid spread of news on social media today makes it essential to distinguish real events from false ones in order to make informed decisions and maintain a healthy transparent society. Case in point is a bizarre story of aman named Ricky Hollowell who was allegedly kidnapped for 3 days by a dolphin named “Gerold “ off the coast of Fort Myers beach near Sanibel Causeway Florida. According to the tale, Mr Hollowell was taken by “Gerold”, a dolphin, for three days to build an underground city. When Mr Hollowells was attended by urgent care he was found dehydrated but with no severe injuries. Although this is an interesting story, this is also entirely fabricated information by a fakebook account page called “The dark humor report”. This story later became viral and spread throughout other social media like X, Instagram, and tiktok with other individuals making jokes referencing these alleged events. Another event that viralized was the alleged claim about the alleged death of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s, this case also through social media and was circulated world wide, but the Israeli government officially dismissed it as fake news. About 86% of global online citizens are exposed to false news online. This is because it is engineered to look real and capture users' attraction. At Lincoln through a survey conducted by The Railsplitter 44% of 50 students surveyed believed that the story of Ricky Hallowell may have happened. Out of 73 students surveyed 50.7% of the students claim they check to see if the information they see on social media is real or not. This highlights how easily misinformation can spread and the importance of questioning sensational stories online in a world where viral content thrives, critical thinking and fact checking are our best tools to separate truth from fiction.
