articles.title: INA One Billion Reasons ZS to Fill Out a Bracket
articles.author: Ryan Lu
articles.dek: Baseball Is Back and the Dodgers
articles.body:
March Madness 2026 has officially begun, bringing the annual excitement of college basketball’s biggest tournament along with a new level of attention on brackets. The 68 team NCAA tournament started after Selection Sunday on March 15 and the main games tipped off March 19, beginning three weeks of single elimination matchups that will end with the national championship in early April. At the center of the tournament is the bracket, where fans predict the outcome of every game from the first round all the way to the championship. Filling out a bracket has become one of the most popular traditions in sports, even though predicting every game correctly is nearly impossible. A perfect bracket requires picking all 63 games correctly, something that has never been done, and the odds are often compared to winning the lottery or being struck by lightning. This year, brackets are getting more attention than ever because of a massive prize. Prediction market platform Kalshi is offering one billion dollars to anyone who can create a perfect bracket for the 2026 tournament. The contest is free to enter, limited to one bracket per person, and requires correctly predicting all 63 games. If no one achieves a perfect bracket, the best overall entry will still win one million dollars. A survey conducted by the Railsplitter highlights just how unlikely most people believe a perfect bracket really is. Out of 73 responses, 52 Lincoln students (71.2%) said they do not believe a perfect bracket will be achieved in their lifetime, while only 21 respondents (28.8%) said they think it is possible. The overwhelming majority of Lincoln students are skeptical, reflecting the widely accepted idea that predicting every game correctly is extremely unrealistic. These opinions line up with the staggering odds. Statistically, a perfect bracket has a 1 in 9.2 quintillion chance (9,200,000,000,000,000,000) if every game is chosen at random, though the odds improve slightly with basketball knowledge. Still, the unpredictability of upsets and buzzer-beaters make perfection incredibly difficult to accomplish. Even so, the fact that nearly 30% of respondents still believe it could happen shows the optimism and excitement that define March Madness. With millions of brackets filled out each year and growing incentives like the billion-dollar prize, fans continue to hope that someday, someone might finally beat the odds, and, as the tournament’s saying goes, expect the unexpected.

Photo courtesy of MLB.com

After almost five months since the World Series, Major League Baseball is back. The 2026 regular season begins “Opening Night” on Wednesday, March 25 where the New York Yankees traveled to San Francisco to face the Giants at Oracle Park. This is the first year when Netflix streamed the game at 5:00 pm. The game also featured the first use of the Automatic Ball-Strike (ABS) system in a Major League contest. Caballero tapped his helmet to challenge a fourth-inning called strike from Webb, which was confirmed. “T wanted to go for it,” Caballero said. “I thought it was a little higher than what it showed, but at least it was close.” The following day Thursday, March 26 all eyes were on Dodger Stadium as they come from winning back to back World Series titles. The Dodgers started their season off by playing their National League West Division rivals the Arizona Diamondbacks for a three game series at Dodger stadium, the first game on Thursday started at 5:30 pm. World Series MVP Yoshinobu Yamamoto took the mound for the Dodgers and taking the mound for the Diamondbacks was Zac Gallen. The Dodgers opened the season in strong fashion, completing a series sweep of the Arizona Diamondbacks as they continue their pursuit of a third consecutive World Series title. The start of the season marks a clean slate for all thirty teams, but for the Dodgers this season can be the one that really cements their history as one of the greatest dynasties in baseball if they win it all in October.

Team USA loses 3-2 in a dramatic final in Miami. By Ruby Campos

Venezuela won their first-ever World Baseball Classic (WBC) title in 2026, defeating Team USA 3-2 in a dramatic final in Miami. The World Baseball Classic showcased the best of international baseball and brought together the best 20 national teams from around the world to complete all in one tournament. The competitions were held from March 5th to the 17th across Tokyo, San Juan, Houston, and Miami. The WBC featured some of the best players from in the MLB to 17 yr olds showing out. The competition is divided into 4 different stages. The first stage of the tournament is called pool play where the 20 teams are split into 4 pools of 5. The teams that played in pool A were Puerto Rico, Cuba, Canada, Panama, Colombia and they all played in San Juan. Pool B had teams like USA, Italy, Mexico, Great Britain, Brazil and played in Houston. Pool C had Japan, Netherlands, Australia, Korea, Czechia and played in Japan. Lastly pool D had Dominican Republic, Venezuela, Israel, Nicaragua, Chinese Taipei playing in Miami. Ultimately pool play determines which teams will advance. Each team faces every other team in their group and only the top two teams from each pool moves on based on wins and for tie breakers it depends on who has the best run differential The second stage of the WBC was the quarter finals where the eight teams that advanced played in a single elimination game. With matchups like the Dominican Republic vs. South Korea, the United States vs. Canada, Italy vs. Puerto Rico, and Japan vs. Venezuela. The Dominican Republic and the USA moved on as expected. While Venezuela shocked the world by defeating the defending champions Japan. Italy also kept their surprising winning streak alive by winning their match ups. The third stage of the WBC is the semifinals where the remaining 4 teams played each other to see who will advance to the championship game. Venezuela continued its historic streak by defeating Italy to finally advance to their first WBC final ever. Meanwhile the USA beat the Dominican Republic in a close match to earn its 3rd straight trip to the championship game In the final stage of the WBC Venezuela stunned the USA with a 3-2 victory at LoanDepot Park in Miami to win its first WBC title ever. Venezuela looked to lead early on and the USA did briefly tie the game but Venezuela did untimely have a key go ahead rbi in the 9th inning giving them the win. The win marked a historic moment for Venezuela baseball and capped an exciting tournament full of upsets and dramatic moments.

articles.body:
LAUSD has updated their new phone ban rules. Lincoln has had a phone free school day which means students are no longer allowed to be on their phones during school hours. This ban covers phones and earbuds

and they may only be allowed before and after school.

Some may ask why LAUSD did this. There are three main reasons which are mental health issues like addiction to the devices, bullying, and the reduction of class distractions. Kids no longer have the opportunity to just be kids,” said Nick Melvoin, the LAUSD school board member who authored a resolution calling for the policy. “I’m hoping this resolution will help students not only focus in class, but also give them a chance to interact and engage more with each other — and just be kids.” Some students may still use their phones without getting caught and others do get caught and get the consequences. “LAUSD offered schools different ways to implement the ban and set aside $7 million for lockers, pouches and other devices to separate students from their phones.” In some schools this rule is enforced and there is no way out of it. Karla Jose Antonio said, “I don't really support the idea of banning phones because English is not my first language and whenever I want to translate something I won't always have my chromebook with me, meaning I won't be able to use google translate.”

On Saturday, December 13, 2025, MST seniors showcased their finalized research projects at Lincoln during the annual MST Senior Symposium. This event serves as the culmination of the Math, Science, and Technology Magnet’s semester-long Science and Technology Research course, also known as Senior Seminar, where seniors present the findings of their in-depth research. The symposium welcomed a wide audience, including MST alumni, professionals from various fields, staff members, parents, fellow MST students, and even the Executive Director of LAUSD, who participated in grading the seniors’ presentations. Each student selected a research topic driven by personal interests, curiosity, or real-world issues affecting themselves or their communities. This year’s projects explored a diverse range of subjects, such as the relationship between voice pitch and human attraction, the science behind different cooking methods, and the design and effectiveness of flood barrier models. The day-long program began with a morning session of slide presentations held in the auditorium. In the afternoon, students presented their work through interactive poster-board displays in the free speech area. Lunch was also provided, featuring pasta, salad, and other light snacks. The symposium concluded with a final round of slide presentations. Reflecting on the symposium, Mr. Te, the new MST chemistry and computer science teacher who co-directed the event alongside Ms. Kwan, described the experience as “really great,” noting how rewarding it was to help students develop their projects and witness their finalized work. Ms. Kwan shared that there is a “possibility” of extending Senior Seminar into a full-year course in the future, allowing upcoming seniors even more time to deepen their research. If implemented, this change would mark an exciting new chapter for the MST program.

It was that time of year again when the Lincoln band and_ orchestra played an amazing performance at the Winter Concert. It had taken Lincoln nearly two months to perfect all their songs with the little practice sessions they had. Not only did the advance band make an appearance, there was also the concert band and the beginning band appeared on stage. The drum line played the Christmas parade sequence walking down the auditorium and making the whole room echo with the sounds of the holiday season. Of course, the orchestra had two amazing pieces of music that created a calm atmosphere. Mr. Sanchez said, “All in all, they did well especially not having a lot of students practicing or showing up. They only messed up a few times and yet we all ended up together in the end.” Gabriela Gomez, a freshman from orchestra said, "They didn't have a lot of adrenaline and the pieces were both easy and hard. The practice before made the event calmer and it felt weird to play with the whole orchestra. Kaden Meza, a Freshman from drumline said, “Tt felt really nice going down the aisle and performing.” From the railsplitter survey 34 of the 218 students said they went to the Christmas Concert and 48 people of the 119 people from another survey said it was just “Okay.”

On December 7th 2025 the 20th annual Lincoln parade was held on Broadway in. The parade had many members of the community such as the schools around the area. The JROTC, band and cheer

from Lincoln HS participated in the parade with JROTC being the 2nd lead in the parade. The parade consisted of people from around the community of Lincoln Heights, such as the Lincoln Heights

Youth Center, The Cheer Center, and even Licha’s Flowers. The opening for the parade was the Aztec dancers, with their ritual dance to bless the parade. There were some performances as well, with kids

dancing to songs from Wicked and others dancing to Cha Cha Real Smooth songs. The parade began from Lincoln Park Ave and ending on Workman, once the parade was over people went to the Lincoln

Heights recreation center park, where they were giving toys away and pizza as well.

A student from Lincoln Heights Youth Center who does boxing, participated in the parade and said that “It was a fun experience yet tiring since it was hot and we included some workouts to make it

fun.” Because of this Christmas annual parade, Lincoln Heights is more recognized, shown by many news websites.

Students at Lincoln have the opportunity to join a wide variety of clubs, which generally fall into three categories: leadership clubs, arts clubs, and cultural clubs. For the Latino community at Lincoln,

MEChA (Movimiento Estudiantil Chicano de Aztlan) serves as an important cultural and activist space. MEChA was founded in 1969 during the Chicano Civil Rights Movement at a conference in Santa Barbara, California, where students came together to promote self-determination, cultural pride, and educational empowerment. The club also has strong roots in the 1968 East Los Angeles Walkouts, where students from Lincoln High School played a key role in protesting educational inequality. Today, MEChA continues to promote Chicano culture, empowerment, political action, and higher education. Currently, MEChA collaborates with members of the Lincoln Heights Neighborhood Council and participates in events that educate others about its history and ancestral heritage. This year, the club has a brand-new cabinet with a renewed goal of giving back to the community. One of these efforts included attending a protest alongside the Neighborhood Council to stop gentrifiers from buying Flat Top, one of the last green spaces in Lincoln Heights. The land is at risk of being sold to wealthy developers to build houses. With the help of MEChA and other community members, petitions were signed to slow down the destruction of this important and beautiful area. In addition to community activism, MEChA has also volunteered at Dia de los Muertos events to honor the people who participated in the 1968 walkouts and to recognize their role in the fight for educational justice. These events also help educate people who may not be familiar with this history, allowing the club to share the importance of cultural memory, resistance, and community awareness. To better understand what MEChA means to its members, Aholibama, the vice president of the club, was asked why they joined MEChA. Aholibama explained, “I joined MEChA to become more educated about our community, our history, and the issues that affect us. I wanted to learn more and be part of a space that values awareness and empowerment.” When asked about their contributions, Aholibama shared that they help support MECHA by participating in school events such as club fairs and by baking goods that are sold for fundraising. They added that they try to contribute wherever help is needed. Finally, when asked why others should join MEChA, Aholibama said the club helps educate people about the Latino community and keeps members informed about what is happening within it. According to them, MEChA is a great way to stay involved, learn more, and support one another.

On June 11, 2026 a sports event will take place in Mexico, Canada, and USA to determine which team will win and receive the highest honor they could ever have. This honor is known as the World Cup Championship. The World Cup is organized by a group called FIFA which stands for International Federation of Association Football. According to the FIFA website Fifa has existed for 122 years. It began on May 21, 1904. It was originally run by seven nations: France, Belgium, Denmark , Netherlands, Spain and Switzerland. Later, FIFA grew beyond Europe, reaching South America and the US. When FIFA first began, it just governed football and oversaw the organizations holding the tournaments. In 1930, the World Cup games were initiated by FIFA’s President Jules Rimet. The first ever World Cup was hosted in Uruguay, featuring 13 teams. Once the World Cup gained fame, FIFA started to expand starting its first Women's World Cup in 1991. Today FIFA now controls major events such as the Men's World cup, Women's World cup, and Club World cup. The World Cup occurs every four years. Before the World cup begins, FIFA prepares

organizations around the world such as, UEFA (Union of European Football Associations), CONMEBOL (the South American Football Confederation), CONCACAF (Confederation of North, Central American and Caribbean Association Football. Once a team earns a spot in the World cup, the teams get split up into different group stages A-L. Each stage consists of four teams. This team I'm sure will catch our attention this year with their passion for the game.

Once a team reaches the highest points they receive a spot in the playoffs. After this is the the quarter-finals, semi-finals, and last but not least the final where a team can be rewarded the World Cup. Top teams that most fans hope to see win this year's Cup are Spain, Argentina, France, England, and Brazil. Argentina is the reigning champions from 2022’s World Cup. However, most fans believe that Spain will be the winner of the World Cup. This is due to their young and strong team. Now France and England have a possibility of winning the world cup because both teams have the most experience playing in the World Cup and because of their world class players. Last but not least Brazil is most likely to win the World Cup due to their new coach, Carlo Ancelotti. He brings immense tactical flexibility. The most anticipated players to watch this Cup are Messi, Ronaldo, Kylian Mbappé, Christian Pulisic. Messi being the reigning MVP of the world cup champion 2022, his skills are unparalleled. Now even though it has been four years Messi is always the same and hopefully makes a two time champ. His rival is Ronaldo, and he and Ronaldo are competing to be the GOAT of the world. Ronaldo is a great player to watch because he is the all-time goal scorer of the world. He has elite skills as he has an elite team to play with him. In this tournament we have a player that always wants to be the best, which is Kylian Mbappé. Mbappé has an entirely new level of physical dominance. His speed is incredible. He knows where and how to move the ball. Overall his performance is top notch. Lastly, Pulisic is always helping and leading the USA team. His positioning and tactical experience is outstanding. He always has constant high efficiency when scoring. Despite the World Cup being such a global hit, FIFA has experienced a few controversies over the years such as, political inferences and biases, corruption & bribery scandals, human right concerns, tournament hosting integrity. This is something to think about as the games approach.

Arising from this hope is the belief in superstitions by some. New Year's superstitions are primarily rooted in the idea that what you do on the first day of the year sets the pattern for the next 365 days. They serve as symbolic rituals to gain a sense of control over the future, focusing on attracting prosperity, finding love, or warding off misfortune. While these customs are rooted in folklore rather than fact, they remain a meaningful way to reflect on the past year and enter the next with intention, hope and a sense of continuity with generations past. After ten years together, Big Sean and Jhené Aiko have decided to call a breakup. Reporters from billboard articles say the breakup was from a disagreement over marriage, with Jhené ultimately walking away after realizing a proposal wasn't coming. Jhene Aiko confirmed that they have separated but have remained friends and the break up was drama free. Big Sean and Jhené Aiko are two of the biggest names in music today. Sean is a famous rapper from Detroit, and Jhené is a popular singer known for her smooth, soulful voice. Together, they were more than just a couple; they were a musical team. They even formed a group called TWENTY88, where they sang

Preparations for the annual New Year’s Rose Parade in Pasadena begins from the very start of the year to the end. Weeks after the parade, organizers are already creating new designs for the following year. Even though it takes all year long to build the floats, the fresh roses brought in from South America mostly are placed just a week before the event. The Tournament of the Roses started as a tradition in 1890. It began when Charles Frederick Holder led a club called Valley Hunt Club, The substantial quantity of flowers influenced the club to include a parade of floral

responsible for the first sponsorship for this event .

decorated carriages before the competition. Years later, the old parade transformed into technologically advanced floats accompanied by marching bands and equestrian groups that travel five and a half miles down Colorado Blvd. The 2026 Rose parade theme for the Pasadena Tournament of Roses was “The Magic in Teamwork”, which celebrated how working together creates extraordinary experiences and achievements. The parade was a showcase of many colorful floats, loud marching bands, and equestrian units. The day of the parade was filled with a significant amount of rain which caused some floats to fall apart. Based on a survey sent out to Lincoln students, the majority did not watch the parade. For one junior that did, she stated that the parade was “very colorful and contained a variation of different roses.” Overall, the Rose Parade is a longstanding tradition that brings together organizers, performers and spectators to celebrate New Year’s.

and rapped about their real-life feelings. In the world of celebrities, where relationships often last only a few months, ten years is a very long time. Fans grew up listening to their music and watching them support each other. Because they were so open about their love, many people felt personally connected to them. Their story is relatable because it deals with a common human struggle: loving someone deeply but realizing you want different things for the future. They are currently focused on peacefully co-parenting their three-year-old son, Noah, while keeping things respectful. Both artists are now putting their energy back into their music, with Jhené recently surprise-dropping a new single titled "Break" and Sean expanding his role as the Creative Director for the Detroit Pistons.
